summary: Microsoft documents the Restart apps sign-in feature, and a guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed the raw RestartApps registry mapping under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon. The current app writes 0 there, which the probe confirmed disables automatic app restart after sign-in on this build.

validation_proof.notes: Guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed the baseline missing state and the 1 / 0 toggle sequence, then restored the machine back to missing.

setting.name: Restart Apps After Sign-In
setting.area: Feature Setting vs Observed Registry Setting
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can automatically save certain restartable apps and reopen them after you sign back in. People turn it off when they want a cleaner sign-in and do not want apps reopening on their own.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to reduce sign-in clutter, control what launches after logon, or avoid apps reopening unexpectedly.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off can make sign-in feel cleaner, but it also removes the convenience of apps automatically reopening where supported. The current product issue is that the app writes a registry value whose raw mapping is now runtime-validated.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents the Restart apps feature in Sign-in options, and the guest probe confirmed the raw RestartApps mapping on Win25H2Clean. Value 0 disables automatic app restart after sign-in, value 1 enables it, and missing means the value is not configured.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The registry value is absent and Windows uses the feature's default Restart apps behavior for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Restart apps off
allowed_values.meaning: Disables automatic app restart after sign-in. The current app writes 0 here and the guest probe confirmed that 0 is the off state.

allowed_values.label: Restart apps on
allowed_values.meaning: Enables automatic app restart after sign-in. The guest probe confirmed that 1 is the on state.

targets.notes: RestartApps is now runtime-validated on Win25H2Clean. Value 0 disables automatic app restart after sign-in, value 1 enables it, and missing means the value is not configured.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes RestartApps = 0, and the guest probe confirmed that 0 disables automatic app restart after sign-in on Win25H2Clean.

windows_defaults.label: Windows feature baseline observed in guest
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows user profiles exposing the Restart apps feature

states.rationale: Guest baseline was missing before the probe and was restored to missing afterward.

windows_defaults.notes: The raw registry baseline on Win25H2Clean was missing before and after the reversible probe.

recommended_profiles.label: Turn off Restart Apps
intended_for: People who want a cleaner sign-in experience
Users who do not want apps reopening automatically after sign-in
avoid_for: Users who rely on supported apps reopening automatically after sign-in

states.rationale: Value 0 disables automatic app restart after sign-in.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want apps to stay closed across sign-in.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want supported apps to reopen automatically after sign-in.
tradeoffs: Automatic app restart after sign-in is disabled.
This is reversible by writing 1 or removing the value.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
intended_for: Users who want the feature left at its default configured state
recommended_profiles.good_when: You do not want to publish an explicit RestartApps policy value.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need to explicitly disable restart apps.
tradeoffs: Windows uses the baseline documented behavior for the current user.

evidence.title: Microsoft support article for the Restart apps sign-in feature
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the user-facing Restart apps feature in Sign-in options, including what it does and how users turn it on or off.

evidence.title: Guest reversible probe - RestartApps registry mapping
evidence.summary: Guest reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed RestartApps transitions through missing, 1, and 0 before restoring to missing.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes RestartApps = 0 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed the raw RestartApps registry mapping: missing, 1, and 0 are all observable states, and 0 disables automatic app restart after sign-in. The current app write now matches the observed off state.
